Title: Finding high-quality content in social media
EvaluationMetrics: auc/roc/auc-roc; cohen's kappa; f1 score; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Uses expert editor judgments to label questions and answers for well-formedness, readability, utility, interestingness, and correctness, serving as ground truth for supervised learning.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Applies supervised classification using stochastic gradient boosted decision trees to integrate heterogeneous feature sets and distinguish high-quality content from lower-quality items.]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[Extracts lexical, syntactic, semantic, and grammatical features from text, including n-grams, readability indicators, language-model divergence, POS tagging, and stylistic markers for quality classification.]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[Constructs multi-relational graphs of users, questions, and answers, and derives graph-based features (e.g., PageRank, HITS hub/authority scores) to model contributor reputation, interaction dynamics, and their influence on content quality.]

Title: Evaluating and Predicting Answer Quality in Community QA
EvaluationMetrics: accuracy; chi-squared test; likert scale; pseudo-r-squared; t-test;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Employs crowdsourced human assessment (Amazon Mechanical Turk) to rate answers across 13 quality dimensions using Likert-scale judgments as ground truth.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Uses supervised logistic regression models to predict whether an answer will be selected as the best answer by the asker based on human-rated aspects and automatically extracted features.]

Miscellaneous
[Applies stratified sampling and extensive validation using 10-fold cross-validation and cross-dataset testing to assess robustness and generalizability of prediction models.]

Title: Selection of the Best Answer in CQA Services
EvaluationMetrics: logistic regression(beta value - ?; odds ratio; omnibus tests of model coefficients; p-value; significance - ?; standard error - s.e; wald statistics - wald;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Employs expert evaluation by domain specialists to identify best answers and to rate answers on content-appraisal dimensions, with inter-rater agreement validated using Cohen’s Kappa.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Applies binary logistic regression to model the likelihood of an answer being selected as the best based on combined social, textual, and content-appraisal features.]

Title: Automatic Identification of Best Answers in Online Enquiry Communities
EvaluationMetrics: auc/roc/auc-roc; f1 score; infogain; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Ranks feature importance using Information Gain Ratio to quantify the relative contribution of content, user, and thread features to best-answer prediction.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Applies a supervised multi-class alternating decision tree classifier to predict best answers across different communities using combined content, user, and thread feature sets.]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[Compares learning algorithms and feature subsets by evaluating precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC–AUC across multiple community datasets.]

Miscellaneous
[Employs standard 10-fold cross-validation to assess model robustness and generalizability.]

Title: Predictors of High-Quality Answers
EvaluationMetrics: beta value Â– b; cohen's kappa; cox and snell r square; hosmer-lemeshow test; log likelihood; nagelkerke r square; odds ratio; significance Â– p;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Applies expert judgment to evaluate content appraisal features and to identify high-quality versus non-high-quality answers, with inter-rater agreement validated using Cohen’s Kappa.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Uses supervised logistic regression to estimate the likelihood of an answer being high quality based on combined social, textual, and content appraisal features.]

Title: Exploiting User Feedback to Learn to Rank Answers in Q&A Forums: A Case Study with Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Analyzes feature importance using information gain to quantify discriminative power across feature groups.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Employs ensemble tree models for learning to rank, including Random Forests and boosted decision-tree variants (EXQD, SHAH, AdTree), trained to predict continuous answer ratings.]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[Incorporates user-graph expertise signals (e.g., ExpertiseRank/PageRank and HITS-style authority) to model relational influence among contributors.]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[Applies learning-to-rank (L2R) with IR relevance functions (BM25) and evaluates rankings using NDCG with cross-validation and statistical significance testing.]

Title: Fit or Unfit: Analysis and Prediction of 'closed Questions' on Stack Overflow
EvaluationMetrics: accuracy; auc/roc/auc-roc; f1 score; pearson correlation;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Performs correlation and temporal trend analyses to examine the evolution, causes, and community dynamics of closed questions over time.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Trains multiple supervised classifiers—including stochastic gradient boosted trees, support vector machines, naive Bayes, logistic regression, and ensemble learners—to predict question closure at creation time, with boosted ensembles achieving the best performance.]

Title: Answering questions about unanswered questions of Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Conducts manual qualitative analysis on a random sample of unanswered questions to derive a taxonomy explaining why questions remain unanswered.]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Applies random sampling and descriptive statistics to analyze distributions of unanswered questions across users, time, and reputation levels.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Trains supervised classifiers, including Random Forests and other machine-learning models, to predict unanswered duration categories for questions.]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[Applies data mining techniques over Stack Overflow posts, metadata, and user histories to extract predictive features.]

Title: A discriminative model approach for suggesting tags automatically for stack overflow questions
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[Trains independent discriminative classifiers using Support Vector Machines for each popular tag, learning decision boundaries that distinguish tag-relevant questions from non-relevant ones.]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[Processes question text through tokenization, stop-word removal, and term-frequency weighting, using MALLET to generate high-dimensional feature vectors for discriminative model training.]

Title: Analyzing question quality through intersubjectivity: world views and objective assessments of questions on social question-answering
EvaluationMetrics: anova; beta coefficients; krippendorff's alpha; kurtosis; mean; r-square; skewness; standard deviation;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Applies Likert-scale coding by trained human assessors to rate multiple dimensions of question quality derived from a grounded typology.]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Uses stratified random sampling, inter-rater reliability assessment (Krippendorff’s alpha), correlation analysis, and multiple regression to relate subjective ratings to objective textual features.]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[Employs data mining techniques to extract and analyze large sets of textual features from question subjects and contents for explanatory modeling.]

Title: From bad to good: an investigation of question quality and transformation
EvaluationMetrics: cohen's kappa; mean; standard deviation; standard error mean; t-test;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Uses human expert assessments by trained reference librarians to label questions as “Bad,” revise them to “Good,” and evaluate quality based on a grounded typology of question failure.]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Applies paired statistical tests and feature comparisons to identify significant differences in textual features before and after revision and to analyze how feature relationships change through transformation.]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[Employs data mining techniques to extract and analyze textual features from original and revised questions for comparative analysis.]

Title: Discovering high quality answers in community question answering archives using a hierarchy of classifiers
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Uses manual annotation of question–answer pairs to label answer quality (good vs. bad) and question types, with high inter-annotator agreement.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Applies supervised logistic regression and other classifiers within a hierarchical framework, where question-type classifiers guide the selection and aggregation of answer-quality classifiers.]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[Performs question-type classification and feature extraction using lexical, syntactic, and semantic NLP techniques, including n-grams, parsing-based features, and translation-based similarity.]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[Conducts feature ranking and similarity analysis (e.g., cosine similarity) and evaluates models using accuracy-based comparisons across question types.]

Miscellaneous
[Employs k-fold cross-validation on natural and balanced datasets to assess robustness and generalizability.]

Title: "A Bit of Code": How the Stack Overflow Community Creates Quality Postings
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Performs large-scale quantitative analysis over Stack Overflow data dumps, including distribution analysis and visualization of code–text ratios, readability scores, sentiment scores, and their relationships with post scores, favorites, and accepted answers.]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[Applies automated text processing to strip code and markup, compute readability metrics, and evaluate sentiment polarity on the remaining natural-language content.]

Title: Understanding and Classifying the Quality of Technical Forum Questions
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[Applies supervised learning models, including decision trees and other classifiers, with genetic algorithms used for feature selection and optimization.]

Miscellaneous
[Uses k-fold cross-validation to evaluate classification robustness and generalizability.]

Title: Chaff from the wheat : Characterization and modeling of deleted questions on Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Conducts longitudinal and temporal trend analyses over multi-year Stack Overflow data snapshots to characterize deletion dynamics and moderation behavior.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Builds supervised predictive models using ensemble-based classifiers (AdaBoost with decision trees) and other machine-learning techniques to predict question deletion at creation time.]

Miscellaneous
[Applies balanced sampling and k-fold cross-validation to evaluate prediction robustness and mitigate class imbalance.]

Title: Questioning the Question -- Addressing the Answerability of Questions in Community Question-Answering
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Uses human assessments collected via crowdsourcing (Mechanical Turk) to label questions as good or bad and to provide quality baselines for model training.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Trains supervised classifiers, primarily Support Vector Machines, to predict question answerability based on extracted textual features.]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[Applies cosine similarity measures to quantify subject–content alignment and support relevance-based feature analysis.]

Miscellaneous
[Evaluates model robustness using k-fold cross-validation on balanced subsets to mitigate class imbalance.]

Title: An Insight into the Unresolved Questions at Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Performs exploratory popularity and comparative analyses between resolved and unresolved questions to identify distinguishing behavioral and topical patterns.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Builds supervised prediction models using J48 decision trees, logistic regression, and Naive Bayes classifiers to predict unresolved questions based on selected metrics.]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[Applies lexical analysis and topic modeling using MALLET (LDA) to extract topic similarity and entropy features from questions and answers.]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[Analyzes historical user behavior and interaction patterns from Stack Overflow metadata to derive predictive behavioral features.]

Miscellaneous
[Evaluates model robustness using k-fold cross-validation across balanced datasets.]

Title: Evaluating Answer Quality across Knowledge Domains: Using Textual and Non-Textual Features in Social Q&A
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Applies exploratory factor analysis using maximum likelihood estimation and varimax rotation to identify latent feature groupings and compare their importance across knowledge domains.]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[Conducts cross-domain comparisons to analyze how the contribution of textual and non-textual features to answer quality differs among science, technology, art, and recreation domains.]

Title: Mining Successful Answers in Stack Overflow
EvaluationMetrics: auc/roc/auc-roc; coefficient estimate; f1 score; odds ratio; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Performs temporal and ablation analyses to quantify the contribution of timing, presentation, affect, and reputation to acceptance likelihood.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Trains supervised classifiers, primarily logistic regression, to predict answer acceptance based on the combined feature set.]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[Applies sentiment analysis (e.g., lexicon-based scoring) to extract affective features from answer text and comments.]

Miscellaneous
[Implements experiments and evaluations using the Weka toolkit.]

Title: Automatic assessments of code explanations: Predicting answering times on stack overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Conducts large-scale quantitative and correlation analyses to examine the relationship between code–text explanatory alignment and time-to-answer across hundreds of thousands of posts.]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[Applies token similarity algorithms, L-ROUGE metrics, and term-based strategies, including grammar-based term extraction using ANTLR4, to quantify explanatory overlap between code snippets and textual descriptions.]

Title: Quality Questions Need Quality Code: Classifying Code Fragments on Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Conducts manual inspection of code fragments to identify constructs indicative of low-quality questions and to derive domain-specific code quality metrics.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Applies supervised classifiers, including Random Forests, Decision Trees, and Logistic Regression, to predict question quality using code-derived features.]

Program and Code Analysis
[Extracts and computes code readability and structural metrics from Java code fragments embedded in questions to quantify code quality.]

Title: Utilizing Content Moderators to Investigate Critical Factors for Assessing the Quality of Answers on Brainly, Social Learning Q&A Platform for Students: A Pilot Study
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Conducts a pilot user study with active Brainly content moderators, employing content analysis and open-discussion evaluations to elicit subjective quality criteria.]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Applies descriptive statistical analysis over Likert-scale survey responses to identify and rank the relative importance of answer quality factors.]

Title: Predicting the quality of questions on Stackoverflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Applies regularized linear models, specifically Ridge regression, to predict question quality while handling high-dimensional and correlated feature spaces.]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[Performs term extraction and semantic analysis using tokenization and stop-word removal to model lexical signals that distinguish high- and low-quality questions.]

Title: Answers or no answers: Studying question answerability in Stack Overflow
EvaluationMetrics: accuracy; cohen's kappa; cox and snell incremental r^2; cox and snell r^2; gelkerke r^2; hosmer-lemeshow test; mean; na; nagelkerke incremental r^2; odds ratio; omnibus test ?2; standard deviation;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Conducts human content analysis with trained coders to rate linguistic errors, completeness, complexity, politeness, and subjectivity using Likert-scale judgments.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Applies hierarchical logistic regression to model question answerability by incrementally incorporating control variables, metadata features, and content-based quality indicators.]

Title: TagCombine: Recommending Tags to Contents in Software Information Sites
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[Formulates tag recommendation as a multi-label classification problem and applies supervised multi-label learning algorithms to estimate tag relevance scores.]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[Transforms textual content using TF-IDF representations and computes tag–term affinity scores to model relationships between terms in content and associated tags.]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[Combines multi-label ranking, similarity-based ranking, and tag–term–based ranking components to produce final tag rankings and evaluates effectiveness using recall-based metrics.]

Title: Detecting high-quality posts in community question answering sites
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Analyzes correlations between question and answer voting outcomes to validate the assumption that high-quality questions tend to attract high-quality answers.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Proposes co-prediction algorithms that jointly learn question and answer quality using logistic regression optimized via gradient descent to exploit voting-score dependencies.]

Title: Building a parsimonious model for identifying best answers using interaction history in community Q&A
EvaluationMetrics: accuracy; auc/roc/auc-roc; infogain; krippendorff's alpha; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Performs statistical analysis and feature ranking (e.g., information gain and correlation analysis) to assess redundancy and predictive contribution of textual and interaction features.]

Machine Learning (ML)
[Applies Bayesian Network (BayesNet) classifiers and supervised learning to build a two-step prediction model that first uses textual features and then refines failed cases using interaction-history features.]

Title: Multi-Factor Duplicate Question Detection in Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[Applies Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA)–based topic modeling to capture latent semantic structures in question titles and descriptions.]

Heuristic and Rule-Based Approaches
[Employs a greedy weight-optimization strategy and a customized composition algorithm to combine multiple similarity factors into a unified duplicate-detection score.]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[Uses cosine similarity–based measures and ranking-oriented evaluation metrics (e.g., recall@k) to assess the effectiveness of duplicate question retrieval.]

Title: Synonym Suggestion for Tags on Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Conducts manual analysis of approved Stack Overflow tag synonym pairs and a user study with developers to derive synonym-creation strategies and to validate the quality of suggested synonyms.]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[Implements multiple synonym-creation strategies based on lexical, phonetic, and structural properties of tags, including stemming, string similarity, acronym handling, phonetic encoding (Metaphone), and token-based matching.]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[Applies data mining over large-scale Stack Overflow tag datasets to extract synonym patterns and to support automated synonym suggestion and ranking.]

Title: CSNIPPEX: Automated Synthesis of Compilable Code Snippets from Q&A Sites
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[Performs large-scale statistical analysis over hundreds of thousands of Stack Overflow posts to quantify the prevalence of uncompilable snippets and to evaluate synthesis effectiveness and precision.]

Heuristic and Rule-Based Approaches
[Introduces the clustering hypothesis to guide greedy inference of import declarations and compilation-unit composition, prioritizing dependency solutions that form coherent package clusters.]

Program and Code Analysis
[Implements compilation-unit inference, external dependency resolution, feedback-directed compilation, code completion, and quick-fix application to iteratively transform raw snippets into compilable Java programs.]

Title: Evaluating the Quality of Educational Answers in Community Question-Answering
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[Applies supervised classification models, including Decision Trees and Random Forests, to predict answer quality using combined personal, community, textual, and contextual feature sets.]

Title: Improving the Quality of Code Snippets in Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[Conducts expert-driven qualitative analysis and a controlled user study to compare original Stack Overflow code snippets with improved snippets generated using the proposed approach.]

Program and Code Analysis
[Applies feedback-driven code improvement through iterative compilation, program slicing to recover missing context, and clone detection to identify and enhance relevant code fragments.]

Title: Mining Duplicate Questions in Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[manual inspection of sampled duplicate and master question pairs to identify and categorize underlying reasons for question duplication]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[statistical analysis of lexical overlap and similarity distributions across large-scale Stack Overflow question pairs]

Machine Learning (ML)
[supervised discriminative classification using logistic regression trained on similarity-based features to predict whether a pair of questions is duplicate]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[application of named entity recognition to extract entities and entity types from question titles and bodies for overlap-based similarity computation]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[use of BM25 ranking to retrieve candidate duplicate questions followed by cosine similarity–based scoring and recall@k evaluation to assess duplicate detection performance]

Title: Predicting Questions' Scores on Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[applies correlation analysis, nonparametric statistical tests, and multiple linear regression to identify factors significantly associated with question scores]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[uses sentiment analysis techniques to compute polarity and subjectivity values from cleaned question text]

Title: Social Question Answering: Textual, User, and Network Features for Best Answer Prediction
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[employs supervised learning-to-rank models, including Random Forests, Logistic Regression, ListNet, and RankSVM, to rank candidate answers by likelihood of being selected as best]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[applies topic clustering and large-scale linguistic analysis to group questions and evaluate feature effectiveness across different question types]

Title: Understanding Question Quality through Affective Aspect in Q&A Site
EvaluationMetrics: f1 score; precision; recall; scott-knott (feature ranking);
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[builds supervised classification models using Random Forests to predict whether a question will receive an accepted answer based on combined feature sets]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[applies sentiment analysis and politeness extraction techniques to derive affective features from question text]

Miscellaneous
[employs repeated 10-fold cross-validation and feature importance analysis with Scott–Knott clustering to evaluate model performance and rank influential features]

Title: Embedded Emotion-based Classification of Stack Overflow Questions Towards the Question Quality Prediction
EvaluationMetrics: accuracy; f1 score; mann-whitney u-test results; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[trains supervised classifiers, including Multilayer Perceptron and Support Vector Machine, to classify questions as high or low quality based on sentiment and content features]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[applies NLP preprocessing, sentiment analysis, and term-frequency analysis to extract TF–IDF–weighted indicator terms and sentence-level sentiment features]

Miscellaneous
[evaluates model performance using 10-fold cross-validation across multiple experimental settings]

Title: It Takes Two to Tango: Deleted Stack Overflow Question Prediction with Text and Meta Features
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[employs probabilistic text classifiers and ensemble learners, including Multinomial Naive Bayes and Complement Naive Bayes, combined with a weighted composite classifier for deletion prediction]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[applies large-scale text mining techniques such as tokenization, stop-word removal, stemming, and term-feature extraction across titles, bodies, and tags]

Miscellaneous
[evaluates prediction performance using longitudinal cross-validation with automated threshold and weight tuning]

Title: By the Community & For the Community: A Deep Learning Approach to Assist Collaborative Editing in Q&A Sites
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[conducts a formative study and user survey with trusted contributors and a field study with a novice editor to understand edit motivations, categories, and perceived usefulness]

Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning
[formulates sentence editing as a sequence-to-sequence learning problem and trains a character-level RNN encoder–decoder model on millions of original–edited sentence pairs]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[applies sentence alignment and normalization techniques to extract parallel sentence pairs and handle domain-specific terms such as APIs, URLs, and code identifiers]

Title: Detecting Duplicate Posts in Programming QA Communities via Latent Semantics and Association Rules
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[trains and compares multiple supervised classifiers to distinguish duplicate and non-duplicate question pairs using combined semantic and association-based features]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[extracts lexical and latent semantic representations through TF–IDF vectorization, neural word and document embeddings, and topic modeling tailored for short technical texts]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[mines association rules and phrase pairs from large sets of manually labeled duplicate questions using alignment techniques adapted from machine translation]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[computes cosine similarity between vector and topic representations and evaluates duplicate detection performance using recall-, F1-, and AUC-based ranking metrics]

Title: Discerning the Quality of Questions in Educational Q&A using Textual Features
EvaluationMetrics: accuracy; auc/roc/auc-roc; f1 score; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[employs human assessment with Likert-scale ratings to label question quality across several qualitative dimensions]

Machine Learning (ML)
[trains supervised classifiers, including SVM, Random Forest, and logistic regression, to predict question quality labels from textual features]

Miscellaneous
[evaluates classification robustness using cross-validation to ensure generalizability of results]

Title: TAGme: A Topical Folksonomy Based Collaborative Filtering for Tag Recommendation in Community Sites
EvaluationMetrics: cosine similarity; f1 score; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[applies item-based collaborative filtering over topic–tag and user–topic profile matrices to recommend tags aligned with users’ previous topical histories]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[employs topic modeling and topical folksonomy construction using TF–IDF, LDA-based variants (including Tag-LDA), and relational topic modeling to capture semantic structure of posts and tags]

Title: Identifying Textual Features of High-Quality Questions: An Empirical Study on Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[applies variable clustering and redundancy analysis to control multicollinearity, followed by readability and descriptive statistics to characterize textual differences between high- and low-quality questions]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[uses sentiment analysis to extract polarity and sentiment strength from question text]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[employs all-relevant feature selection and feature importance ranking using data-mining techniques such as the Boruta algorithm]

Title: Scalable tag recommendation for software information sites
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[formulates tag recommendation as a multi-class classification problem and applies an efficient classification algorithm to assign multiple relevant tags to new software objects]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[computes semantic similarity between software objects using normalized textual representations and tag normalization to handle synonymous and noisy tags]

Heuristic and Rule-Based Approaches
[filters unreliable and low-frequency tags using predefined thresholds to reduce noise and stabilize the tag space]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[ranks candidate tags based on similarity-weighted scores derived from semantically related software objects and evaluates recommendations using precision-, recall-, and F1-based ranking metrics]

Title: Towards Question Improvement on Knowledge Sharing Platforms: A Stack Overflow Case Study
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[trains predictive models using SVM, logistic regression, kNN, and decision trees to estimate whether edited closed questions will be successfully reopened]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[performs extensive feature engineering over textual, formatting, and orthographic attributes of question titles, bodies, and tags]

Title: Question's advisor a wizard interface to teach novice programmers how to post "better" questions in stack overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[conducts user studies and usability testing with programmers to elicit desirable question characteristics and to assess the perceived usefulness of the wizard interface]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[applies NLP techniques to analyze draft questions, detect missing desirable characteristics, and generate actionable, real-time suggestions for question improvement]

Title: On early detection of high voted Q&A on Stack Overflow
EvaluationMetrics: accuracy; auc/roc/auc-roc; f1 score; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[applies logistic regression as a baseline and extends it with relational classifiers and joint prediction models that simultaneously infer question quality, answer quality, and acceptance likelihood]

Title: The best answer prediction by exploiting heterogeneous data on software development Q&A forum
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[analyzes user background knowledge by modeling historical answering behavior, reputation, and expertise indicators as a complementary view to textual similarity]

Machine Learning (ML)
[formulates best-answer prediction as a multi-view learning problem and applies the BoostStack ensemble framework to combine predictors trained on text, code, and user knowledge views]

Title: Language Use Matters: Analysis of the Linguistic Structure of Question Texts Can Characterize Answerability in Quora
EvaluationMetrics: accuracy; auc/roc/auc-roc; f1 score; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[conducts psycholinguistic analysis to interpret how linguistic and emotional cues correspond to perceived question answerability]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[computes diversity and distribution statistics over part-of-speech tags to quantify structural variation in question texts]

Machine Learning (ML)
[trains supervised classifiers, including SVM, logistic regression, and random forest models, to predict whether a question will be answered within a given time window]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[extracts linguistic features using LIWC categories, topic distributions from LDA, and edit-distance–based similarity via ROUGE-LCS recall]

Title: Understanding Stack Overflow Code Fragments
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[conducts a survey-based user study with open-ended questions and qualitative coding to identify recurring issues that hinder code fragment understandability]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[uses stratified sampling and descriptive statistical analysis of self-explanatory ratings to quantify perceived code understandability across topics]

Title: Are Code Examples on an Online Q&A Forum Reliable? A Study of API Misuse on Stack Overflow
EvaluationMetrics: diversity score; likert scale; manual validation; relevance score; usefulness score;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[performs manual inspection of sampled Stack Overflow posts to validate detected API misuses and assess their potential runtime impact, supported by inter-rater agreement]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[mines frequent API usage patterns, call sequences, and guard conditions from hundreds of thousands of GitHub repositories using subsequence mining and predicate equivalence analysis]

Program and Code Analysis
[applies program slicing and structured call-sequence extraction to isolate API-relevant code fragments and improve the precision of usage pattern mining]

Title: Data-Driven Proactive Policy Assurance of Post Quality in Community Q&A Sites
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[conducts a large-scale empirical analysis of collaborative editing logs to identify common editing types and mid-level quality assurance practices manifested through community edits]

Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning
[formulates post-edit recommendation as a text classification problem and applies a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to learn editing patterns and predict required edit types]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[uses text classification over post bodies with word embeddings and CNN-based feature learning to detect quality issues and localize key phrases that justify edit recommendations]

Title: Duplicate Detection in Programming Question Answering Communities
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[trains and evaluates a wide range of supervised classifiers, including instance-based, linear, kernel-based, ensemble, and neural models, to predict whether a question pair is duplicate]

Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning
[leverages neural representation learning to model latent semantic similarity between questions, enabling more robust detection beyond lexical overlap]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[extracts vector-based semantic representations, applies probabilistic language models with smoothing, and uses topic modeling to characterize question similarity]

Heuristic and Rule-Based Approaches
[filters and prunes candidate questions using tag overlap and ranking heuristics to reduce the search space before classification]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[mines frequently co-occurring phrase pairs from known duplicate questions to construct association features that capture domain-specific paraphrasing patterns]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[employs IR-style ranking models (e.g., BM25 and language-model–based scoring) to select top-k candidate duplicates and evaluates performance using recall-at-k and classification metrics]

Title: Two Perspectives on Software Documentation Quality in Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[manually validates extracted documentation-quality indicators and their relationships with answer acceptance and voting outcomes]

Machine Learning (ML)
[applies a Naďve Bayes classifier to predict whether answers will be accepted or highly voted based on combinations of documentation-related features]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[performs automated readability analysis to derive linguistic features used in predictive modeling]

Title: Determinants of quality, latency, and amount of Stack Overflow answers about recent Android APIs
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[conducts manual case studies to validate whether detected API mentions are central to questions and to characterize how new APIs are discussed]

Machine Learning (ML)
[applies regression-based predictive models, including logistic regression, Poisson regression, and linear regression, to estimate effects on answer quality, quantity, and latency]

Heuristic and Rule-Based Approaches
[filters and cleans large-scale Stack Overflow and Android API datasets to control for confounds such as deleted posts, migrated questions, and noisy API links]

Miscellaneous
[analyzes the role of bounties as an incentive mechanism by modeling their timing and presence in relation to answer quality and response speed]

Title: ICSD: An Automatic System for Insecure Code Snippet Detection in Stack Overflow over Heterogeneous Information Network
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[manually labels code snippets as secure or insecure based on established Android security principles and expert inspection to construct ground-truth datasets]

Machine Learning (ML)
[applies a multi-view fusion classifier that integrates heterogeneous representations learned from multiple relational views to predict insecure code snippets]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[learns low-dimensional representations of code snippets using network embedding over heterogeneous graphs through the Snippet2Vec model]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[constructs and mines a heterogeneous information network using meta-path–guided random walks to encode rich semantic relationships among users, posts, code, and keywords]

Title: EnTagRec ++: An enhanced tag recommendation system for software information sites
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[models tag–term and tag–object relationships using Bayesian inference (e.g., labeled topic modeling) and frequentist inference based on tag co-occurrence and term statistics]

Machine Learning (ML)
[applies grid search–based parameter tuning to optimally combine multiple inference components (Bayesian, frequentist, user-based, and initial-tag–based) into a unified tag-ranking model]

Title: Exploiting User Activities for Answer Ranking in Q&A Forums
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[formulates answer selection as a learning-to-rank problem and applies ranking algorithms including SVMRank, PageRank, and HITS to combine textual, user, comment, and network-based features]

Title: Guiding supervised topic modeling for content based tag recommendation
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML)
[employs a supervised generative topic model that constrains topic assignments using known tags to directly guide tag prediction]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[applies topic modeling with LDA and supervised variants, augmented with Word2Vec-based semantic similarity to identify content words relevant to tags]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[evaluates tag recommendation effectiveness by ranking candidate tags according to learned topic–content relevance and measuring recall at top-k positions]

Title: How to ask for technical help? Evidence-based guidelines for writing questions on Stack Overflow
EvaluationMetrics: auc/roc/auc-roc; coefficient estimate; odds ratio; p-value;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[conducts user surveys and qualitative content analysis to elicit community norms and perceptions of what constitutes a good technical question]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[applies logistic regression analysis to estimate the statistical association between question features and the likelihood of receiving answers]

Machine Learning (ML)
[uses logistic regression with clustering-based discretization to model nonlinear effects of continuous features]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[applies sentiment analysis techniques to extract affective features from question text]

Title: Finding and Ranking High-Quality Answers in Community Question Answering Sites
EvaluationMetrics: auc/roc/auc-roc; f1 score; matching_top10; precision; recall; rmse;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[addresses class imbalance by applying random under-sampling to construct balanced training datasets]

Machine Learning (ML)
[trains ensemble and probabilistic classifiers, including Naive Bayes, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting, to distinguish and rank high-quality answers]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[analyzes feature importance and contribution using ranking-based evaluation to identify the most influential predictors of answer quality]

Title: FastTagRec: fast tag recommendation for software information sites
EvaluationMetrics: f1 score; manual evaluation; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[conducts qualitative analysis to understand tagging behavior and guide feature design for efficient tag recommendation]

Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning
[employs a neural network with a softmax output layer to model tag prediction as a multi-class classification problem]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[represents post content using bag-of-words and n-gram features, combined with Huffman coding for efficient text encoding]

Title: AnswerBot: An Answer Summary Generation Tool Based on Stack Overflow
EvaluationMetrics: diversity score; likert scale; manual validation; relevance score; usefulness score;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[conducts controlled user studies to compare generated answer summaries against baseline search-engine–based summaries in terms of relevance, usefulness, and diversity]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[leverages bag-of-words representations, Word2Vec embeddings, inverse document frequency weighting, maximal marginal relevance for diversity-aware selection, sentence pattern recognition, and user-oriented feature extraction]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[mines large-scale Stack Overflow data to retrieve relevant questions, extract candidate answer paragraphs, and support scalable summary generation]

Title: Features That Predict the Acceptability of Java and JavaScript Answers on Stack Overflow
EvaluationMetrics: auc/roc/auc-roc; matthews correlation coefficient; neural network coefficient; random forest coefficient;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[addresses class imbalance by applying synthetic oversampling techniques, including SMOTE and ADASYN, and evaluates feature relevance using information gain]

Machine Learning (ML)
[trains Random Forest and Neural Network models to predict answer acceptability and identify features with the highest predictive power]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[performs text preprocessing and tokenization to enable similarity computation, sentiment extraction, and feature construction from answer content]

Title: How Reliable is the Crowdsourced Knowledge of Security Implementation?
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[conducts qualitative inspection of security-related posts and analyzes community feedback to understand how insecure implementations are discussed, corrected, or propagated]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis
[applies statistical analysis to quantify the prevalence and distribution of insecure security practices across different cryptographic categories]

Program and Code Analysis
[uses clone detection techniques to identify recurring insecure code patterns and track their replication across multiple Stack Overflow posts]

Title: Integrating Multi-Level Tag Recommendation with External Knowledge Bases for Automatic Question Answering
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis
[analyzes tag semantics and usage patterns to characterize hierarchical tag levels and to qualitatively assess the relevance of recommended tags and retrieved knowledge base articles]

Machine Learning (ML)
[employs supervised multi-class classification models trained on post–tag co-clusters to predict relevant tag clusters for new questions, combined with neighborhood-based voting to recommend final tags]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing
[uses topic modeling with Latent Dirichlet Allocation to represent posts and tag descriptions in a low-dimensional semantic space, enabling scalable similarity computation and clustering]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models
[constructs post–tag bipartite graphs and applies multi-level spectral co-clustering to uncover hierarchical tag structures, while mining external knowledge bases using recommended tags for targeted article retrieval]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation
[ranks retrieved knowledge base articles using tag-augmented tf-idf representations and cosine similarity, and evaluates retrieval effectiveness using recall, coverage, precision, mean reciprocal rank, and normalized discounted cumulative gain]

Title: SOTagRec: A Combined Tag Recommendation Approach for Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML) [applies collaborative filtering based on TF-IDF representations and cosine similarity to estimate tag relevance from historically similar questions, and combines probabilistic outputs from multiple models using weighted linear aggregation]

Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning [employs a convolutional neural network with word embeddings and multiple convolution layers to learn deep semantic representations of question text and predict independent tag probabilities in a multi-label setting]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [represents questions using word embeddings trained on Stack Overflow data and TF-IDF vectors after tokenization, stop-word removal, and stemming to support both neural and similarity-based models]

Miscellaneous [uses ten-fold cross-validation on large-scale Stack Overflow datasets to tune model parameters and evaluate tag recommendation effectiveness using Recall@k and Precision@k]

Title: Towards an Accurate Prediction of the Question Quality on Stack Overflow using a Deep-Learning-Based NLP Approach
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning [employs a deep neural network architecture consisting of a gated recurrent unit (GRU) followed by multiple dense layers to perform binary classification of question quality based solely on textual representations] 

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [applies NLP preprocessing including text cleaning, tokenization, and vectorization, and represents questions using document-level embeddings derived from spaCy vectors and Stack-Overflow-specific Doc2Vec models to capture semantic properties of posts]

Title: Detecting Duplicate Questions in Stack Overflow via Deep Learning Approaches
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning [employs multiple deep neural architectures, including CNN-, RNN-, and LSTM-based models, to learn non-linear semantic representations of question pairs and predict duplication likelihood through probability ranking]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [constructs duplicate and non-duplicate question pairs and represents question text using distributed word embeddings to capture semantic similarity for duplicate pair detection]

Title: Predicting the Timing and Quality of Responses in Online Discussion Forums
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML) [employs logistic regression for answer likelihood prediction and fully connected neural networks optimized via gradient descent to predict response quality based on engineered user–question features]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [uses topic modeling with Latent Dirichlet Allocation to infer latent discussion topics from question text and support computation of topical similarity features]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models [constructs question–answer and denser user interaction graphs to extract centrality, similarity, and resource-allocation features, and applies point process models to capture temporal dynamics of user responses]

Title: An empirical study of obsolete answers on stack overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis [manually inspects sampled obsolete answer threads, associated comments, and edit histories to understand obsolescence reasons, user reactions, evidence provided, and post-observation maintenance actions]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis [analyzes large-scale Stack Overflow data to measure the prevalence of obsolete answers, time-to-obsolescence detection, update frequency, tag-level obsolescence ratios, and statistical differences across communities]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [classifies reasons for answer obsolescence by analyzing textual cues in comments and answer content, categorizing causes such as API evolution, programming language changes, outdated references, tools, platforms, and protocols]

Heuristic and Rule-Based Approaches [applies keyword-based heuristics on comments to automatically identify candidate obsolete answers and construct a large dataset for subsequent quantitative and qualitative analysis]

Title: Quality assessment of answers with user-identified criteria and data-driven features in social Q&A
Categorized_Methodology_Details: Quantitative and Statistical Analysis [applies large-scale quantitative analysis combining exploratory factor analysis and linear regression to identify dominant quality dimensions and examine how textual and non-textual features correlate with answer scores across domains]

Title: Assessing the quality of answers autonomously in community question-answering
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML) [builds supervised classification models using logistic regression, decision trees, and random forests to predict answer quality from heterogeneous feature sets]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models [extracts egonet and graph-structural features from user interaction networks to model social influence, connectivity, and information flow patterns relevant to answer quality prediction]

Title: Code Duplication on Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis [conducts manual inspection of representative duplicated code snippets to understand reuse patterns, clone contexts, sources, and maintenance behaviors]

Program and Code Analysis [applies code normalization and fingerprinting to detect exact and non-trivial code clones across threads, analyzes clone distribution and evolution, and examines licensing information to identify copyright and attribution conflicts]

Title: Generating Question Titles for Stack Overflow from Mined Code Snippets
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis [conducts human evaluation to assess the naturalness, clarity, and relevance of generated question titles, and to examine their effectiveness in improving low-quality questions]

Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning [employs an LSTM-based sequence-to-sequence architecture enhanced with attention, copy, and coverage mechanisms to model code-to-text generation and handle rare tokens and repetition in generated titles]

Title: Large-Scale Question Tagging via Joint Question-Topic Embedding Learning
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [learns shared semantic representations of questions and tags by embedding both into a common latent space to enable similarity-based matching]

Machine Learning (ML) [uses a multilayer perceptron to model interactions between learned question and tag embeddings and support large-scale tag prediction]

Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning [employs convolutional neural networks to encode question text, integrates hierarchical directed acyclic graph encoding of tags, optimizes predictions using sigmoid cross-entropy loss, and applies transfer learning to handle sparse and imbalanced tags]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation [ranks candidate tags by computing similarity scores between question and tag embeddings and evaluates effectiveness using top-k ranking–based matching metrics]

Title: Why Will My Question Be Closed? NLP-Based Pre-Submission Predictions of Question Closing Reasons on Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis [balances highly skewed class distributions through downsampling to ensure reliable training and evaluation of binary and multi-class prediction models]

Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning [employs recurrent neural network architectures based on gated recurrent units, including unidirectional, bidirectional, and composite GRU-based models, to predict question closure and specific closing reasons]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [applies text cleaning, tokenization, stemming, and part-of-speech tagging to preprocess question text and extract linguistic features used as input for neural models]

Title: Duplicate Question Detection With Deep Learning in Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [represents question text using Word2Vec embeddings to capture word-level semantic information for similarity learning]

Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning [employs deep neural architectures including CNN-, RNN-, and LSTM-based models built on Word2Vec representations to learn semantic similarity between question pairs and predict duplication likelihood]

Title: Improving Quality of a Post's Set of Answers in Stack Overflow
EvaluationMetrics: accuracy; completeness; comprehensibility; conciseness; correctness; f1 score; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis [relies on human judgment to label posts as deficient or non-deficient by manually evaluating answer sets against defined quality criteria]

Machine Learning (ML) [trains supervised classification models, including neural networks, decision trees, and support vector machines, to predict posts that require improvement based on post-level features]

Program and Code Analysis [applies clone detection and program slicing techniques to extract and recommend relevant code snippets that help contributors generate higher-quality answers]

Miscellaneous [implements an IDE-based prompting tool to proactively suggest deficient posts to developers and support answer improvement with minimal context switching]

Title: Predicting closed questions on community question answering sites using convolutional neural network
EvaluationMetrics: f1 score; micro-receiver operating characteristic (roc) curve; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Quantitative and Statistical Analysis [addresses severe class imbalance in question status labels by applying synthetic minority oversampling techniques to improve the robustness of prediction models]

Machine Learning (ML) [builds baseline classifiers using traditional algorithms such as naďve Bayes, gradient boosting, random forest, and k-nearest neighbor trained on handcrafted linguistic features]

Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning [employs convolutional neural network and long short-term memory architectures to automatically learn contextual and semantic representations from question text and perform multi-class closure reason prediction]

Title: TagDC: A tag recommendation method for software information sites with a combination of deep learning and collaborative filtering
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning [leverages a hybrid neural architecture combining Bi-LSTM for contextual word representation, convolutional neural networks for local feature extraction, and capsule networks to model multi-label tag confidence]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [represents software object descriptions using distributed word and document embeddings to capture both word-level semantics and global contextual meaning]

Data Mining and Graph-Based Models [applies collaborative filtering to identify semantically similar historical software objects and reuse their associated tags to complement deep learning–based predictions]

Title: Multilayer Convolutional Neural Network to Filter Low Quality Content from Quora
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Machine Learning (ML) [trains traditional classifiers using handcrafted linguistic features to establish baseline performance for low-quality content detection]

Deep Learning (DL) / Transformer-Based Deep Learning [employs a multilayer convolutional neural network to automatically learn hidden contextual patterns from question text and classify posts as low-quality or acceptable]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [represents questions using distributed word embeddings generated through skip-gram, continuous bag-of-words, and pre-trained embedding models to support semantic learning]

Miscellaneous [applies k-fold cross-validation to ensure robust evaluation and reduce bias arising from fixed train–test splits]

Title: Understanding stack overflow code quality: A recommendation of caution
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis [conducts in-depth manual inspection of representative code snippets and their surrounding discussion context to understand the practical implications of detected quality issues]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis [performs large-scale quantitative analysis and correlation studies to measure the prevalence, distribution, and co-occurrence of different code quality violations]

Program and Code Analysis [applies static analysis tools to detect reliability issues, readability violations, performance inefficiencies, and security vulnerabilities in sampled code snippets, combined with systematic snippet sampling and violation categorization]

Title: Toxic code snippets on stack overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis [conducts user studies involving Stack Overflow answerers and visitors to understand awareness, perceptions, and behaviors related to reusing outdated or license-violating code snippets]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis [performs large-scale empirical analysis to quantify the prevalence of toxic code snippets, their reuse frequency, and the extent of outdated and problematic solutions]

Program and Code Analysis [applies clone detection to identify reused code between Stack Overflow and open-source projects, classifies toxic code types, and analyzes outdated clone evolution and license conflicts]

Title: Are Comments on Stack Overflow Well Organized for Easy Retrieval by Developers?
EvaluationMetrics: accuracy; auc/roc/auc-roc; cosine similarity; precision; recall;
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis [manually categorizes comments through open coding to identify informative versus uninformative content types, such as improvements, weaknesses, inquiries, and additions]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis [performs large-scale empirical analysis and statistical testing to compare characteristics of hidden and shown comments and assess the effectiveness of the existing organization mechanism]

Machine Learning (ML) [trains supervised classifiers to automatically distinguish informative comments from uninformative ones using textual, activity, and user-related features]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [applies vector space models and text preprocessing to compute similarity between answers and comments and support classifier feature construction]

Relevance, Ranking, and Evaluation [evaluates alternative comment organization strategies by ranking comments using similarity measures and classifier predictions to improve retrieval of informative comments]

Title: Early Detection and Guidelines to Improve Unanswered Questions on Stack Overflow
Categorized_Methodology_Details:
Qualitative Analysis [manually inspects answered and unanswered questions to identify recurring deficiencies in problem description, topic assignment, code explanation, and formatting, and to derive actionable improvement guidelines]

Quantitative and Statistical Analysis [conducts large-scale comparative analysis using random sampling and statistical testing to identify significant differences between answered and unanswered questions across multiple features]

Machine Learning (ML) [builds supervised prediction models using decision trees, random forests, k-nearest neighbors, and neural networks to identify unanswered questions during submission based on available features]

Text Analysis and Natural Language Processing [applies readability analysis and topic entropy modeling to quantify linguistic clarity, topical focus, and information density of question text]

Title: Quantifying Human-Perceived Answer Utility in Non-Factoid Question Answering
